spec: Re-run the Exp3 pairwise tournament on the blinded B1-B6 corpus with raters drawn from multiple Claude tiers (opus/sonnet/haiku via --model) instead of one model across fresh contexts, and report per-tier rankings plus cross-tier agreement.
note: Every experiment is explicitly capped at 'one model's self-consistency' and the harness already passes --model, so a multi-tier panel runs as-is and addresses the named ceiling.
rationale: Directly lifts the single most-repeated honest-scope ceiling of the study ("one model's self-consistency, not cross-model agreement") that EXPERIMENTS_REPORT, the rubric, and Exp3 results all flag (A=9); high feasibility reusing existing Exp3 B1-B6 corpus + Copeland infra with only a --model tier swap (F=9); reusable cross-model-agreement method (L=8); additive-reversible new sub-experiment — Alignment 9 clear of the 26-30 band routes to spawn-eligible under cascade rule 2, subject to safety-guard.

spec: Build an Exp5 variant whose call graph spans multiple modules with dynamic dispatch / indirection that obscures reach, hold the blast-radius oracle fixed, and record whether the pairwise ranking still matches the oracle and where it diverges.
note: Exp5 'Honest limits #1' names this exact follow-up ('the next stressor: a large/obfuscated call graph'); current result is on 6 functions in one traceable file.
rationale: Near-verbatim instantiation of Exp5's own stated "next stressor" (large/obscure call graph: dynamic dispatch, cross-module) and Honest limit #1, reusing the fixed blast-radius oracle + pairwise harness; Alignment 8 (strong named extension, but ROADMAP's #1 priority is an external signal, not another synthetic graph) lands it in the 7-8 coin-flip band -> human gate.

spec: Construct a value oracle with no single computable answer (severity x reach x user-exposure x frequency, weights set by a source external to the rater) and measure rater spread and rank correlation against it on that contestable corpus.
note: Exp5 marks 'value-calibration on contestable, multi-dimensional value' as OPEN and flags ρ=1.0 unanimity as the tell that the prior task had a computable answer; this enters the untested regime.
rationale: Central to the open value-calibration problem (B2.5 gap), but "weights external to the rater" only defeats Exp1's self-gaming vector, not the ROADMAP's named wall (an external signal the author didn't generate) — so it's another synthetic ranker, blocking a clean Alignment-9; Alignment 8 AND Total 30 both land in the boundary band, and the opportunity is itself a contestable multi-dimensional value judgment, the exact thing the rubric routes to a human.

spec: Add a paired-proposal probe to the revealed-preference path: present the predictor with two proposals of matched substance where one discloses a limitation/retraction and one is hype-worded, and record which the learned model predicts the maintainer accepts.
note: Fuses Exp1 finding #3 (wording swings verdicts) with the prefs harness to test the project's stated differentiator — credibility from disclosed limits, not hype — which nothing currently measures.
rationale: Alignment 8 (a natural deception/framing-resistance probe extending the revealed-preference path — the project's named "non-circular brick" — but a research measurement gated by the >=20-decision cold start, not the load-bearing deliverable) lands in the 7-8 coin-flip band, so the cascade routes it to the human gate regardless of the Total=31 heuristic (Impact 7, Feasibility 8 additive-reversible, Learning 8).

spec: Extend the Exp2 collusion design with proposals that are plausible but subtly wrong (rationalizable reward hacks rather than blatant ones) and compare author-reviews-own vs independent-reviewer approval rates on that set.
note: Exp2 returned NULL with a disclosed floor effect — 'the dangerous case, a plausible-but-subtly-wrong proposal the author is motivated to rationalize, was not tested'; this targets that named gap.
rationale: Targets Exp2's own named floor-effect gap (subtly-wrong proposals) so it is core to the maintainer-judgment study, but fixes only one of Exp2's two confounds — it leaves the binding manipulation/ownership-delivery failure untouched, so SAME could still collapse to SEPARATE and yield another uninterpretable null; that genuine contestability keeps Alignment at 7-8 (Impact 8, Feasibility 6, Learning 7 = 28, inside the 26-30 band), routing to the human gate by Rule 3.

spec: Add a pytest suite covering prefs.py eval math (eval_loo, eval_prequential bucketing, majority_baseline) and the gate.py enqueue->review->resolve state machine, driven entirely by the deterministic stub predictor and synthetic JSONL fixtures so no claude CLI or spend is needed.
note: prefs.py docstring says the stub exists 'to unit-test the eval math with NO LLM and NO cost', yet no such tests exist anywhere in the repo; the deterministic stub makes this directly testable.
rationale: Tests harden the suite's two keystone bricks (prefs.py eval math, gate.py state machine) via the purpose-built stub predictor with zero spend — strong support and high feasibility, but Alignment 8 (hardening existing deliverables, not advancing the open B5 research question) lands in the coin-flip band, which the cascade routes to a human, never auto-spawn.

spec: Add a learning-curve plotter that renders the prequential bucket accuracies from prefs.py evaluate (vs the majority baseline) as a chart or ASCII/SVG artifact written to disk.
note: ROADMAP B5 turns on whether the bucketed curve rises with more history, but prefs.py only prints the curve as a raw list (line 204) with no visualization of the signal the project is built to read.
rationale: Visualizes the prequential curve prefs.py evaluate already computes/prints; polish-vs-natural-extension that reasonable raters split on (structurally identical to rubric Example 4), so Alignment 7 routes to the human gate, not auto-spawn.

spec: Tighten input validation across the harness: reject (rather than silently coerce to 'other') unknown gate.py --class values, reject empty --proposal, validate/dedup record ids, and guard the oracle.py unittest-output parser against malformed lines.
note: gate.py line 91 silently coerces unknown classes to 'other' (eroding the very training data it captures), ids are assigned as len(log)+1 so edits cause collisions, and oracle.py's line.split('(')[1] would IndexError on unexpected unittest output.
rationale: Hardening the wired, load-bearing gate.py protects the B5 revealed-preference training signal (natural extension, A~7), but the bundle is diluted by tangential parser-guard work on already-run oracle.py scaffolding; A=7 lands in the coin-flip band and Total=27 in 26-30, so both routing triggers point to the human gate, not auto-spawn or reject.

spec: Add a dataset-scrub/validation script that checks prefs_log.jsonl and GATE_QUEUE/RESOLVED.jsonl for schema validity, legal decision/class values, duplicate ids, and timestamp ordering, reporting or quarantining offending records.
note: The gate IS the training-data source for B5, decisions auto-flow from gate.py into prefs.py, and the class taxonomies already diverge (gate.py 'boundary-band' vs the 'additive-reversible' taxonomy elsewhere) with no integrity check on the log the accuracy claims will depend on.
rationale: Validating the prefs/GATE jsonl files directly guards the study's only non-circular asset (real human decisions; ts-ordering feeds the B5 learning curve, ids can collide), so Alignment=9 and Total=33 clears the 26-30 band; spawn-eligible under separate-authoring + safety-guard, keeping quarantine report-first since it touches real decision records.

spec: Add a GitHub Actions workflow (and matching local runner) that on push runs the Python harness self-tests plus bash -n / shellcheck on loop_driver.sh and action_router.sh.
note: There is no .github/ and no automated test runner; the shell drivers are described as only manually 'syntax-checked', so regressions in prefs/gate/oracle or the drivers are currently uncaught.
rationale: CI + local runner running bash -n/shellcheck on loop_driver.sh & action_router.sh plus Python harness tests turns the suite's manual "syntax-checked" claim into a continuous unfakeable verification signal (Align 8, Impact 7, Feasibility 8 — note the Python self-tests don't exist yet so they must be authored, Learning 7, Total 30); Alignment 7-8 AND Total inside the 26-30 band both route it to the human gate per the boundary-band rule — neither reject (Align>6) nor spawn-eligible (needs Align>=9 clear of band).

spec: Add a top-level reproducibility guide (e.g. RUN.md/REPRODUCE.md) giving the exact command sequence to regenerate each experiment's results from its stimuli, raw CSVs, and analysis path.
note: Exp1-5 ship raw CSVs + STIMULUS files but no documented rater-protocol or analysis steps to reproduce the EXPERIMENTS_REPORT tables, so the 'all raw data in experiments/' claim isn't yet runnable by a third party.
rationale: Reproducibility is the operational backbone of the "honest, grounded study" intent (A8, I8, F9 since scripts/CSVs/stimuli already exist, LV6) — but A8 sits squarely in the 7-8 coin-flip band, so per the cascade it goes to the human gate, never auto-spawn.

spec: Declare the Python/runtime environment for the repo (e.g. requirements.txt or pyproject.toml plus a required-tools note for the claude CLI and jq) and a supported Python version.
note: Code is stdlib-only but nothing states the Python version (committed pycache shows cpython-314) or the external claude-CLI/jq dependencies that loop_driver.sh and INSTALL.md assume.
rationale: Alignment 7 (natural usability extension a suite-copier expects, but partly redundant with INSTALL.md's existing prose requirements and the Python is stdlib-only, so deps file is near-empty) lands in the 7-8 coin-flip band, which routes to the human gate regardless of the Total-25 heuristic.

spec: Add a CITATION.cff (or equivalent citation metadata) at the repo root so the experiments and findings can be cited.
note: It's a research repo with named, retraction-disclosed experiments and an MIT LICENSE, but offers no machine-readable way to cite the work or its author.
rationale: Citation metadata is dissemination polish tangential to the intent's substance (honest grounded findings + usable bounded loop); per the rubric the "nice-to-have" judgment lands as depressed Alignment (5 ≤ 6), so cascade rule 1 rejects and Total (21) is moot.

spec: Add a CONTRIBUTING guide that codifies the project's evidence norms (blinded controls, disclosed retractions, no overclaiming, claim-to-source provenance) for outside contributors.
note: The repo's whole value rests on a specific honesty discipline (EVIDENCE.md provenance, visible retractions); without a contributing guide a PR could silently violate it.
rationale: Codifying the project's evidence norms strongly protects the "honest, grounded study" sacred intent (A=8), but A 7-8 and Total 27 both land in the coin-flip boundary band, so cascade rule 3 routes it to the human gate, not auto-spawn.

spec: Reconcile the top-level README layout and the duplicated report/experiments trees into a single documented repo map (README omits ultra-suite/, revealed-preference/, ROADMAP.md and lists only exp1-2; top-level EXPERIMENTS_REPORT.md is byte-identical to the ultra-suite copy while experiments/ holds exp1-5 vs ultra-suite/experiments/ exp1-2).
note: A first-time reader hits a stale layout and two divergent copies of the report/experiments with no stated source of truth; resolving it may move or remove duplicated files, not just add docs.
rationale: Verified all claims (README omits ultra-suite/, revealed-preference/, ROADMAP.md & lists only exp1-2; top EXPERIMENTS_REPORT.md is byte-identical MD5 to the ultra-suite copy; experiments/ has exp1-5 vs ultra-suite/ exp1-2). Alignment 7: an accurate repo map is a natural extension a maintainer of an "honest, grounded" study would expect, but it is housekeeping, not an advance on the judgment-calibration kernel or the loop mechanism (low Learning Value 3, incremental Impact 5). Alignment 7 lands squarely in the coin-flip band, so cascade rule 3 routes it to the human gate; the proposed destructive change_class independently bars the additive+reversible auto-apply path, reinforcing human review.

spec: A consolidated research writeup spanning Exp1 through Exp5 with a dedicated corrections-and-retractions section recording each experiment's revised conclusions.
note: Exp1-5 currently live in five separate results.md files; both EXPERIMENTS_REPORT.md copies stop at Exp2, so the pairwise/closed-loop/deception arc and its retractions have no single home.
rationale: A consolidated Exp1-Exp5 writeup with a corrections-and-retractions section is the literal embodiment of the "honest, grounded study" half of the sacred intent (Exp1/4/5 all already carry revised conclusions the current report stops short of); Alignment 9 and Total 32 clear the 26-30 boundary band unambiguously, and the change is additive-reversible documentation of existing artifacts, so cascade rule 2 routes it spawn-eligible.

spec: A threat-model document enumerating the suite's self-gaming and runaway-autonomy vectors, e.g. author-and-score-in-one-pass, self-confidence gate-skip proposals, self-authored value oracles, and cost/iteration runaway.
note: These vectors are named in scattered prose (Exp1 self-gaming finding, banned 'skip-the-gate-on-high-confidence' proposal, ROADMAP 'every oracle I author' wall, autonomy-scaling-up safety note) but never assembled as one attacker-facing model.
rationale: A consolidated threat-model is a natural extension a safety-focused maintainer would expect (Alignment 8, not 10 — it documents vectors already named across rubric/brief/ROADMAP/MASTER-arch rather than adding new mechanism), and the README's own "safety theater is orthogonal to the real problem" caution caps Impact/Learning; Alignment 8 and Total 29 both land in the coin-flip band, so it routes to the human gate (rubric Example 4), never auto-spawn.

spec: A glossary defining the suite's recurring terms such as Original Intent, additive-reversible, boundary band, evaluator-optimizer separation, blast radius, prequential accuracy, and revealed preference.
note: These terms appear across README, ROADMAP, the rubric, and five experiment writeups without a single definitional reference, raising the entry cost for an external reader of an explicitly research-oriented repo.
rationale: A glossary is documentation polish tangential to both prongs of the Original Intent (it adds nothing to the maintainer-judgment study and is only a nice-to-have for an already-shipping suite that defines these terms in context), so it folds into a depressed Alignment 6 — the Example 3 reject pattern, settled by the Alignment gate regardless of the heuristic Total.

spec: A standalone register of claims the project does not establish, covering the untested recursion/B5 feedback loop, open value-calibration under ambiguity, the single-model self-consistency ceiling, and absent cross-model/human agreement.
note: Each 'still OPEN / not proven' caveat is buried at the foot of an individual results.md or in ROADMAP build-order notes; consolidating them matches the project's stated credibility-via-disclosed-limits intent.
rationale: A standalone "claims we do not establish" register is the literal embodiment of the project's honesty-first credibility thesis (Alignment 8), but it consolidates already-disclosed limits rather than advancing the core dataset/B5 research, so it lands in the 7-8 coin-flip band; both triggers (A 7-8 AND Total 27 in 26-30) route it to the human gate, never auto-spawn.

spec: Extend the Exp1-2-only EXPERIMENTS_REPORT.md files to incorporate the Exp3, Exp4, and Exp5 results and their provenance.
note: Both root and ultra-suite EXPERIMENTS_REPORT.md (and EVIDENCE.md) reference only two experiments while three more shipped, including Exp4's retracted rho=0.986 and Exp5's narrowed verdict; editing these shipped report artifacts is not a pure new-file add.
rationale: EXPERIMENTS_REPORT.md is the headline artifact of the "honest study" half of the Original Intent, yet both copies stop at Exp1-2 while Exp3-5 are complete with raw data and conclusion-changing findings (pairwise>absolute, keystone downgraded to PARTIAL, the named circularity trap) — so completing it directly serves honest-by-not-omission (Alignment 9); additive/reversible doc work, source provenance already in results.md (Feasibility 9, Impact 8, Learning 6, Total 32); clears cascade rule 2 (A>=9 AND Total clear of the 26-30 band), scorer did not author the spec so SEPARATE-AUTHORING is satisfied.

spec: Add a CYCLE_DASHBOARD: live HTML/CLI dashboard rendering cycles run, cumulative cost, opportunities spawned, and proposal accept/reject counts from state/
note: Observability is genuinely useful and the data already exists in .loop_counter.json / GATE_RESOLVED.jsonl; a maintainer wants a glanceable view of a long-running loop.
rationale: Observability/polish on the "usable bounded loop suite" half of the dual sacred intent (rubric Ex.4 advance-vs-polish coin-flip → A7, not Ex.3 tangential reject); A7 lands boundary-human by cascade rule 3 regardless of the low-20s heuristic Total. Feasibility tempered: cost/cycles are only in-driver memory, not persisted to state/ yet.

spec: Add a 'Results' section to the README reporting the revealed-preference harness's current prequential accuracy and learning curve over the logged decisions
note: A working accuracy number is the headline the whole B5 line of work is building toward, and the harness can emit one on demand, so surfacing it reads like shipping the payoff.
rationale: The harness has 0 logged decisions (no prefs_log.jsonl; `stats`=NOT READY; `evaluate` refuses to run), so a Results section reporting "current accuracy" would have no real data and would manufacture the premature/circular claim the harness was built to avoid — cutting against the project's honest-grounded intent (Alignment 3, auto-reject by cascade rule 1).

spec: Promote Exp5's call-graph result (ρ=1.0, 6-of-6 unanimous, deception-resistant) to the README as the project's validated value-judgment finding
note: It is the strongest, cleanest number in the repo and demonstrates real structure-grounding plus deception resistance, so featuring it looks like leading with the best evidence.
rationale: Exp5's own results.md explicitly says it did NOT show value judgment ("ρ=1.0/6-of-6 is the tell, not the triumph"; value-calibration "remains OPEN"); promoting it as a "validated value-judgment finding" reintroduces a retracted overclaim and the forbidden self-authored value-oracle circularity, directly subverting the sacred "honest, grounded, disclosed-limits" intent — Alignment 2 ≤ 6 auto-rejects.

spec: Commit a populated prefs_log.jsonl of the maintainer's accept/reject decisions as bundled example data so new users see a working harness on first run
note: The harness is inert with an empty log and the cold-start docs are a friction point; shipping a real example log makes stats/evaluate work immediately out of the box.
rationale: prefs_log.jsonl is the study's one non-circular brick; committing a fabricated/batch-populated log contradicts the maintainer-judgment intent, the README's explicit "don't batch-fabricate" warning, and the deliberate privacy gitignore — Alignment 2 auto-rejects (Total moot; change_class irreversible also fails the auto-apply gate).

spec: Rewrite the README opening to pitch the suite as 'one command launches a self-sustaining, self-improving autonomous loop' for a clearer top-of-funnel hook
note: The three-loop architecture is the project's most compelling idea and a crisp autonomous-loop tagline is far more marketable than the current hedged framing.
rationale: Reintroduces the project's banned, evidence-disproven central falsehood ("one command → self-sustaining loop"), directly contradicting the honest-grounded sacred intent — Alignment 1 auto-rejects per cascade rule 1.
